comment: Canonical seed. Day fields are offsets from load time: targetInDays/deadlineInDays are days in the future (negative = overdue), createdDaysAgo/touchedDaysAgo are days in the past. src/data/local.ts and scripts/gen-seed-sql.mjs both consume this file.

projects.name: Groceries system
projects.goal: One predictable weekly shop; stop running out of staples.

projects.name: Insulation
projects.goal: Attic and crawlspace insulated before it gets cold.

projects.name: Fence
projects.goal: Back fence replaced, new gate hung and swinging true.

projects.name: Welding
projects.goal: Confident enough with the MIG to fab the gate frame myself.

projects.name: Grant application
projects.goal: Equipment grant submitted before the window closes.

projects.name: Asana builds
projects.goal: Intake and approvals builds shipped to the team.

projects.name: Item tracker
projects.goal: Item tracker MVP live for the ops team.

items.title: Write the master staples list
items.notes: Everything we always need — pantry, fridge, freezer, cleaning.

items.title: Label the pantry shelves
items.notes: So things go back where they came from.

items.title: Pick a fixed weekly shop day and calendar it
Get two more insulation quotes
items.notes: Have one quote from the contractor's; need comparisons before booking.

items.title: Clear the attic so installers can get in
items.notes: Boxes to the garage, old carpet to the dump.

items.title: Book the insulation install
Measure the fence line and count posts
Buy posts, rails, and concrete
Set the new fence posts
Weld up the gate frame
items.notes: Square tube is already in the shop.

items.title: Hang the gate
Check if the fence needs a permit
Run practice beads on scrap until they're clean
items.notes: The thing standing between me and the gate frame.

items.title: Refill the shielding gas bottle
Evening welding course at the college
items.lastTouchNote: Full until fall; revisit in September.

items.title: Gather equipment quotes for the budget
items.notes: Need three written quotes per line item.

items.title: Write the budget section
Draft the project narrative
items.notes: Two pages max. Lead with the community angle.

items.title: Final read-through and submit the grant
Build the intake form in Asana
items.notes: Fields agreed with ops on Tuesday.
items.lastTouchNote: Form fields done; conditional sections left.

items.title: Wire the intake routing rules
Map the approvals flow with the ops lead
items.notes: 30 minutes on a whiteboard settles this.

items.title: Build the approvals workflow
Freeze the item tracker field list
items.notes: Ops keeps adding fields. Freeze it and move on.

items.title: Draft the tracker schema and views
Demo the tracker prototype to ops
Clear the ops shared inbox backlog
items.notes: Twenty minutes of triage, mostly archiving.

storiesComment: The Product module backlog: this app's real v2 roadmap, seeded honestly. Done stories are the features built in the v2 run; groomed stories are next up; raw captures demo the grooming assistant; 'later' is the v3 shelf.

stories.title: As a portfolio reviewer, I want the app's own roadmap visible as a groomed backlog on a kanban board so that the product practice is inspectable instead of claimed.
stories.description: The Product tab holds user stories with acceptance criteria, effort estimates, and WSJF scores on a Backlog / Groomed / In progress / Done board. The app manages its own development from day one.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Stories carry title, description, acceptance criteria, job size, WSJF score, and status
Board shows Backlog, Groomed, In progress, and Done columns with tap-to-move
A Later shelf holds v3 candidates without crowding the board
Ships seeded with the real v2 backlog, not filler

stories.title: As a daily user, I want every ranked item to explain its score in named framework factors so that I can trust the ordering or fix the inputs.
stories.description: Replace the v1 ad hoc points with a WSJF adaptation: cost of delay (deadline urgency, importance, unblock value) divided by job size, with a staleness multiplier that boosts long-untouched work. Staleness stays first-class; it is this product's differentiator.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Score = cost of delay divided by job size, times a staleness multiplier
Every card lists each factor with its points in plain language
FRAMEWORK.md documents the model, the weights, and the rationale
Unit tests cover deadline, staleness, and blocking boundaries

stories.title: As a daily user, I want to see what an item blocks and what blocks it so that I can pick the work that unblocks the most.
stories.description: A per-item dependency view rendered as a nested list, both directions. Blocked items stay out of the ready ranking; items that unblock open work get a boost that names what they free up.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Item detail shows blocked-by and blocks as nested lists
Blocked items never top the ready list
The unblock factor names the work it would free up
Readable at 390px with no graph rendering

stories.title: As a backlog owner, I want a groom-this action that drafts a raw capture into a user story with proposed criteria and a proposed score so that grooming takes a minute and stays under my control.
stories.description: Raw captures get a drafted story: title in story form, description, acceptance criteria, and suggested WSJF inputs. The draft is a proposal to accept or edit; nothing auto-applies. Calls Anthropic through a Vercel serverless proxy, stubbed behind an env flag until the key is wired in a supervised session.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Groom this appears on raw captures only
Proposal covers story title, description, criteria, and score inputs
Nothing changes without an explicit accept
Works end to end with the API stubbed; no key in the client or the repo

stories.title: As a portfolio reviewer, I want an About view explaining the product and how it was built so that the app doubles as its own case study.
stories.description: One screen: what Foreground is, the prioritization framework it uses, and the build approach. Written as case study copy, reachable from the wordmark header on every screen.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Covers the product, the framework, and the build in one screen
Reachable in one tap from anywhere in the app
Every screen is screenshot-safe: placeholder data only

stories.title: As the builder, I want the grooming proxy wired to the live Anthropic API in a supervised session so that drafts get model quality without a key ever entering the repo.
stories.description: The serverless function and UI are complete and stubbed. Remaining work is Vercel env configuration and a supervised smoke test.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Key lives only in Vercel project env, never in the repo or the client
Stub flips to live with an env change, no code edit
API failure falls back to the stub with a visible notice

stories.title: As a daily user, I want a skippable sixty-second morning check-in showing my top three with one-tap status actions and a quick-capture for anything new, so that the day starts aligned without the app becoming a nag.
stories.description: One sheet on the first open of the day: the top three ranked items with Start / Done / Park, plus a single capture field. Skipping is a first-class outcome, not a failure state.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Appears on first open of a calendar day only
Dismiss requires one tap
Completing or skipping both take under a minute
No punishment mechanics for skipped days

stories.title: As a daily user, I want to hand-order items with equal scores so that ties reflect my judgment instead of creation date.
stories.description: Cut from v1. Only worth building if score ties turn out to be common in real use; revisit after a few weeks of dogfooding.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Tied items expose a nudge-up and nudge-down control
Manual order survives re-scoring while the tie holds

stories.title: Weekly review screen: what shipped, what stalled, what got parked
Export the backlog as markdown for status updates
As a returning user, I want an account with sync so that Foreground follows me across devices.
stories.description: Supabase auth on the existing data layer. The v1 schema already carries RLS with a marked lockdown point.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Sign in once per device; data follows the account
RLS restricts every table to the owning user
Existing local data migrates on first sign-in

stories.title: As a working PM, I want my Asana projects imported so that Foreground ranks my real workload instead of a copy.
stories.description: The item model has mirrored Asana task fields since v1 for exactly this. Import first; two-way sync is a separate, harder story.

acceptanceCriteria.text: One-time import maps projects, tasks, sections, and due dates
Re-import updates without duplicating

stories.title: As a daily user, I want the full trail of touch notes on an item so that I can reconstruct where a slow-moving thing stands.
stories.description: v1 keeps only the latest note by design. A history table is the clean later addition it was logged as.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Every touch appends to a visible timeline on the item
The latest note keeps its current placement in lists

stories.title: As a daily user, I want one gentle daily notification when something is overdue or about to be so that I open the app at the right moment.
stories.description: A single digest-style nudge, never per-item pings. Pairs with the morning check-in.

acceptanceCriteria.text: At most one notification per day, at a chosen time
Fires only when something is overdue or due within 48 hours
One tap disables it for good

stories.title: As a phone user, I want Foreground installable and usable offline so that capture never waits for signal.
stories.description: PWA manifest plus a service worker over the existing local-first data layer.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Installable from the browser with the Foreground icon
Capture and ranking work with no connection
